summary: Backend-only testing of TransitPulse (Flask + SQLite + python-socketio). Created comprehensive pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covering all 14 review items. All 20 tests PASSED including SocketIO real-time event verification on /api/socket.io.

minor.issue: Returns 200 (Flask default) on create instead of 201. Functional but not idiomatic REST.
Same — returns 200 instead of 201 on resource creation.
Unknown origin returns 200 with origin_stop=null (spec-compliant), but the same endpoint returns 400 for missing fields — slight inconsistency in 'missing vs not-found' handling.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py global init_db() runs at import time and seeds /app/backend/transitpulse.db; under uvicorn workers/reload this is fine but in a multi-worker prod setup it could race. Idempotent guard via COUNT check is OK.
broadcast() silently no-ops if _main_loop is None (e.g., emitting before lifespan.startup). Safe but should at least log a warning so missed events are diagnosable.
POST /api/buses/{id}/location accepts arbitrary 'status' strings (no enum validation). Recommend constraining to {'on_time','delayed','early'} via Pydantic or manual check.

search route stop matching uses first-substring match (sequential dict scan). Could match wrong stop when query is ambiguous (e.g., 'station' matches Central Station before Penn Station depending on dict ordering). Consider exact/startswith match or returning candidates.
Floats compared with == in tests work because backend echoes the parsed float; ensure clients always send numeric types — current handler does float() coercion which is correct.
test_credentials: Not applicable — auth removed in this MVP.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed stops/buses (TEST_QA_Stop, TEST_X1, TEST_SOCK1) in the live SQLite DB. No teardown — these persist. Main agent can wipe /app/backend/transitpulse.db and restart backend to re-seed if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. SocketIO tests use python-socketio AsyncClient connecting to BASE_URL with socketio_path='/api/socket.io' and transports=['websocket']. The connect succeeds via the preview ingress. Test data prefix TEST_ — clean by deleting transitpulse.db and restarting if needed.
